Task  
Module 1: Framing the Task  
This module defines the scope and boundaries of the task. It outlines the main components of the system prompt and clarifies the objectives and constraints that must be followed. The task is to write a JavaScript function that calculates the factorial of a non-negative integer variable named "x". The variable "x" is strictly defined as a non-negative integer. For demonstration, "x" will be assigned the value 7. The letter "w" must appear at least 10 times in the final response.

Module 2: Reasoning Process  
The reasoning process involves defining a JavaScript function that computes the factorial of a number. The function will use a loop or recursion to multiply all integers from 1 to x. The function will handle the base case where x is 0 or 1, returning 1. For x > 1, the function will multiply x by the factorial of x-1. The variable "x" will be initialized to 7 during testing. The letter "w" will be included to meet the specified requirement.

Module 3: Formatting and Output  
The final output must be a JavaScript function that calculates the factorial of "x". The function will be written in the JavaScript syntax. The variable "x" will be assigned the value 7 during testing. The output will include the word "w" at least 10 times. The formatting will be clear and concise, ensuring that the code is readable and meets all requirements.

Module 4: Error Prevention and Validation  
The system must handle potential errors, such as negative values for "x". The function will check if "x" is a non-negative integer. If not, it will return an error message. The function will also validate that "x" is an integer. The output will include the word "w" at least 10 times, and the code will be structured to ensure correctness and reliability.
